campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and the current state of the treasury. They also discussed plans to improve how people get around Campbell by developing a multimodal transportation plan that likely includes buses, bikes, walking paths, and other travel options.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City treasury and financial status update
New multimodal transportation plan to improve local travel options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and scheduled it for final approval at their next meeting instead of going through the normal multi-step approval process.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input on a proposed ordinance
Council waived the standard first reading requirement to speed up the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at the next regular council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance topic not provided in available information

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: Los Altos City Council met to finalize the 2026 weed removal program and approve new rules for how the council will operate going forward. The council also made decisions about affordable housing policies, approved raising two community flags, and discussed transportation and housing plans with county transit officials.
keyTopics: Completed 2026 weed abatement program final report
New City Council operating procedures and norms adopted
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Santa Clara Valley transportation and housing implementation updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council approved several contracts and agreements to move forward with ongoing projects. The council authorized the town manager to sign construction work with QLM, Inc., hire professional services from Dudek, and join a statewide insurance program (CJPIA) to help manage the city's liability coverage. Several existing agreements with contractors were also updated with amendments.
keyTopics: Construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Professional services agreement with Dudek
Joining California Joint Powers Insurance Authority (CJPIA)
Multiple contract amendments with existing vendors
Art transfer agreement authorization

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on April 9, 2026 to review how the city is supporting kids and teens through various programs and services. They discussed youth empowerment initiatives, summer safety programs, enforcement of city codes, and plans to improve library facilities and customer service.
keyTopics: Children and Youth Services Master Plan progress update
Safe Summer Initiative Grant and youth intervention programs
Code Enforcement annual report on city violations
Library facility improvements and customer experience upgrades

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents could join the virtual meeting via Zoom or phone to watch proceedings and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access available via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Instructions for raising hand to speak during agenda items

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they started with the Pledge of Allegiance and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their connections to Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous March meeting.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission regular meeting
Personal stories shared by three commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute/Pledge of Allegiance

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives, including a presentation about a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous meeting and opened up time for residents to speak about any city matters.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer jobs program for young residents
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where residents could participate either in person or online via Zoom. The city is making it easier for people to stay involved in local government by offering multiple ways to attend and submit comments about agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom participation available)
Remote access via Zoom with meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Phone dial-in option: 1(669) 900-6833
Online written comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve several financial and construction matters for the city. Key decisions included joining a tax board partnership with the county, considering fee waivers for a local educational organization, awarding a construction contract, and reviewing plans for community development funding.
keyTopics: Tax Board partnership with Franchise Tax Board and county
Fee waiver and donation request from Milpitas Community Educational
Construction project approval and contract award
Community development funding plan amendments
New workplan requests from individual council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss the city's gas utility finances and rates for the next fiscal year. The council reviewed and approved changes to how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service, with different rate adjustments for residential customers, multi-unit buildings, and large commercial users.
keyTopics: Gas utility rate increases for 2027 fiscal year
Approval of new rate schedules for residential and commercial customers
Reserve fund transfers and general fund adjustments
Meeting minutes from March 4 Utilities Advisory Commission
